quranicRelevance: Water (Maa'/Miyah) as the source of life, rain, and purification.
figurativeOrRareUsages: مَاءٍ مَهِينٍ: A despised/weak fluid (semen, representing human physical origins).
discardedNoise: Plating objects with gold/silver (تمويه) - post-classical metaphorical use.
summaryEn: The root refers to water, representing the vital source of all living things, rain, and purification in the Quran.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الماء، وهو يمثل المصدر الأساسي للحياة الطاهرة والري والتطهير في القرآن الكريم.
rawText: أَمَاهَ 4 He (a digger) produced, or fetched out, water, by his labour or work; syn. أَنْبَطَ المَآءَ: (S, K:) or reached the water: (Msb:) or reached much water; as also أَمْهَى. (AA, in TA, art. نبط.) === أَمَاهُ الرَّكِيَّةَ He (a man) produced, or fetched out, by his labour, or work [in digging,] the water of the well; syn. أَنْبَطَ مَآءَهَا: (S, K:) He (God) made the water of the well to be much, or abundant. (Msb.) مَوَّهَ 2 He silvered or gilded, (S, K,) or washed over with gold or silver, (Msb,) a thing (S, Msb, K, TA) of brass (TA) or copper or iron. (S, K.) === He [varnished or] embellished falsehood so as to give it the appearance of truth. (TA.) He falsified information, عَلَيْهِ to him, in reply to a question. (K.) === He involved in confusion, or doubt; or practised concealment or disguise: or he concealed or disguised: (S, TA:) and he deceived, deluded, beguiled, circumvented, or outwitted. (TA.) === He varnished, or embellished with a false colouring.
